title: avena sterilis var. ludoviciana
intro: Avena sterilis var. ludoviciana, also known as ludovic oat grass or wild oat, is a subspecies of the oat grass family and a type of wild oat plant. It is a grass-like plant native to the Americas, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The plant contains a compound called avenanthramides, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Avena sterilis var. ludoviciana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote a good night's sleep and overall well-being.
